project.name: Adaptive Honeypot System
project.description: A production-grade, AI-powered honeypot network with real-time behavioral adaptation and Cognitive-Behavioral Deception Framework (CBDF) for manipulating attacker decision-making.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty init file for AI module package

nodes.name: monitoring.py
nodes.summary: AI Monitoring Service for real-time AI workflow management. Analyzes live events, processes logs, and dynamically reconfigures containers using LLM-based decision making.

nodes.name: decision_executor.py
nodes.summary: Executes AI decisions on Docker containers. Connects AI monitoring decisions to actual honeypot operations including reconfiguration, isolation, and container switching.

nodes.name: analyzer.py
nodes.summary: Enhanced AI Analyzer with multi-provider support, fallback chains, caching, and structured output for attack analysis.

nodes.name: __init__.py

nodes.name: AIActivityStatus

nodes.name: ThreatLevel
nodes.summary: Enum representing threat levels: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL

nodes.name: AIActivity
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single AI activity event with id, timestamp, status, action, details, and metadata

nodes.name: AttackEvent
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing an attack event for analysis with source IP, honeypot ID, attack type, commands, and credentials

nodes.name: AIDecision
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing AI-generated decision for container reconfiguration with threat level, reasoning, and MITRE ATT&CK IDs

nodes.name: LocalLLMClient
nodes.summary: Client for local LLM API (api.ai.oac) with async generate and health_check methods using httpx

nodes.name: AIMonitoringService
nodes.summary: Central AI monitoring service that analyzes live events from honeypots, processes logs using AI/ML, makes dynamic container reconfiguration decisions, and manages container switching

nodes.name: ExecutionStatus
nodes.summary: Enum representing execution status: PENDING, RUNNING, SUCCESS, FAILED, ROLLED_BACK

nodes.name: ExecutionResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing result of a decision execution with id, decision_id, action, status, and error details

nodes.name: DecisionExecutor
nodes.summary: Executes AI decisions on honeypot containers supporting monitor, reconfigure, isolate, and switch_container actions with Docker API integration

nodes.name: EnhancedAIAnalyzer
nodes.summary: Production-grade AI analyzer with multi-provider support, automatic fallback chains, response caching, and structured output parsing

nodes.name: RuleBasedAnalyzer
nodes.summary: Fallback analyzer using rule-based analysis for attack classification when AI providers fail

nodes.name: AIProvider
nodes.summary: Enum representing AI providers: OPENAI, ANTHROPIC, GEMINI, LOCAL

nodes.name: AIResponse
nodes.summary: Dataclass wrapping AI response with content, provider, model, tokens used, and duration

nodes.name: AIAnalysisResult
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for structured AI analysis result with attack sophistication, threat level, deception strategies, and MITRE ATT&CK IDs

nodes.name: AnalysisCache
nodes.summary: Simple in-memory cache for AI responses with TTL-based expiration

nodes.name: OpenAIProvider

nodes.name: AnthropicProvider

nodes.name: GeminiProvider

metadata.module_doc: A novel approach to honeypot deception that exploits human cognitive biases

layers.name: AI Layer
layers.description: AI monitoring service, decision executor, and multi-provider LLM integration (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini)

layers.name: Alerting
layers.description: Multi-channel alerting via email, Slack, Discord, and webhooks

layers.name: API Endpoints
layers.description: REST API endpoints for honeypot management, attacks, sessions, cognitive data, and system configuration

layers.name: API Core
layers.description: FastAPI application setup, middleware, and routing configuration

layers.name: Cognitive Deception
layers.description: Cognitive-Behavioral Deception Framework (CBDF) engine, profiler, and bias detection

layers.name: Data Collectors
layers.description: Log collection from Cowrie honeypots and cognitive integration bridge

layers.name: Core Services
layers.description: Configuration, security, GeoIP lookup, and deployment utilities

layers.description: SQLAlchemy models, repositories, and database session management

layers.description: Base classes for SSH, HTTP, FTP, and Telnet honeypot implementations

layers.name: UI Components

tour.title: Entry Point & Configuration
tour.description: Start with the FastAPI application setup and configuration. The app.py bootstraps the entire backend, setting up middleware, routing, and database connections.

tour.title: Database Models
tour.description: The data layer defines SQLAlchemy models for honeypots, sessions, attack events, cognitive profiles, and deception events. These models power the entire system's persistence.

tour.title: Honeypot Infrastructure
tour.description: Honeypot base classes define the contract for SSH, HTTP, FTP, and Telnet decoys. These integrate with Docker containers managed by the deployment module.

tour.title: Log Collection Pipeline
tour.description: CowrieLogCollector tails honeypot logs, deduplicates events, and routes them to both the AI monitoring service and cognitive integration bridge.

tour.title: AI Monitoring Service
tour.description: The AI layer provides real-time threat analysis using LLM providers. It recommends actions (monitor, reconfigure, isolate) which are executed by the DecisionExecutor.

tour.title: Cognitive Deception Engine
tour.description: The CBDF engine builds psychological profiles of attackers, detects 8 cognitive biases, and applies 11 deception strategies to manipulate attacker behavior.

tour.title: Alerting System
tour.description: Multi-channel alerting supports email, Slack, Discord, and generic webhooks with rate limiting and priority-based formatting.

tour.title: API Endpoints
tour.description: REST endpoints expose honeypot management, attack analytics, cognitive profiles, AI monitoring, and system settings. WebSocket endpoints provide real-time updates.

tour.title: Frontend Dashboard
tour.description: React frontend with TailwindCSS provides dashboards for attack monitoring, cognitive analysis, AI decisions, and honeypot management.

tour.title: Real-time Data Flow
tour.description: WebSocket hooks and React Query provide real-time data flow from backend to frontend, with context providers for auth and notifications.
